figures.caption: Figure 9: Uncurated CIFAR-10 samples from each flow-matching variant after 400,000 training steps, generated with EMA weights and the DOPRI5 adaptive solver from a fixed noise seed x0 „ N p0, Iq. The harmonic family interpolates between OT-CFM (ω Ñ 0) and OT-SI (ω “ π{2) as ω increases. OT-Aniso uses the data-fit anisotropic harmonic potential described in Sec. H.4.

Figure 1: Lagrangian flow matching unifies trajectory selection (top row) and probability-path construction (bottom row) under the leastaction principle, linked by the static–dynamic equivalence (Thm. 2.1). The kinetic Lagrangian (left column) yields straight-line trajectories and recovers OT-based flow matching; a general Lagrangian (right column) yields curved trajectories and induces new probability paths.

Figure 4: Mini-batch harmonic flow matching (ω “ 1) from an 8-Gaussian-mixture source (olive) to a double-moon target (blue), swept over OT batch size n P t1, 10, 25, 50, 100u. Increasing n tightens the empirical coupling toward the OT solution: compare the many-to-many fan-out at n “ 1 to the bundled, mode-to-region routing at n “ 100. Trajectory curvature is set by ω and is preserved across all panels — the two axes (n for the coupling, L for the matched-pair trajectory) act independently.

Figure 8: Sample quality vs. inference budget. We plot the 2-Wasserstein distance W2 between generated and target samples against the number of function evaluations (NFE) at inference, across five sourceÑtarget settings (rows) and three ODE integrators (columns). OT-CFM and OT-Harmonic at ω “ 10´3 are visually indistinguishable, consistent with the harmonic interpolant degenerating to the affine one as ω Ñ 0. OT-Harmonic at ω “ 1 matches or improves on this shared baseline at every NFE budget, with the largest gains in the low-NFE regime where step error dominates. Shaded bands show ˘1 standard deviation across five training seeds.
Figure 6: CIFAR-10 images generated by OT-CFM (left) and OT-Harmonic (right) with frequency ω “ 0.001.

Figure 3: Mini-batch harmonic flow matching trajectories from a 8-Gaussian-mixture to a doublemoon target, swept over ω P p0, π{2s and compared against OT-CFM and the stochastic interpolant. Endpoints (black) are matched by quadratic-cost OT and connected along least-action trajectories (olive); the data manifold is shown in blue. See Examples 2.4 and 2.5 for the limiting cases.
